campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear a financial update from the regional transportation authority about their March investments, discussed a new policy to prevent federal agencies from using city property and facilities, and addressed the city's efforts to fill job openings and keep employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's March 2026 investment report
New policy restricting federal use of city-owned property
City job vacancies and employee retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a hearing where residents could voice concerns about cleaning up hazardous materials on certain properties that the county identified. The council then approved a plan to move forward with these cleanups and adopted new policies about technology and how residents can request accommodations from the city.
keyTopics: Hazardous material cleanup on county-identified properties
New Technology Disruption Policy replacing old rules
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations from the city

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on May 5, 2026 to handle several routine business items. The main focus was addressing overgrown vegetation and brush as a safety hazard, approving a landscape and lighting report, and signing multi-year service agreements with outside contractors.
keyTopics: Declaring overgrown brush and vegetation a public nuisance
Approving engineer's report for landscape and lighting maintenance
Authorizing 5-year service agreements with Pacific and other contractors
Reviewing and approving previous meeting minutes

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 12, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on May 12, 2026 to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes and updating banking arrangements with Wells Fargo. The council also discussed a new financial assistance program to help water customers struggling with bills and approved work with a consulting firm on an unspecified project.
keyTopics: New financial help program for residents with high water bills
Updated banking agreement with Wells Fargo for city finances
Contract work with Kitchell/CEM consulting firm
Routine approval of previous meeting minutes

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 12, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council held a private meeting to discuss two legal matters: a potential lawsuit related to a water main problem at Cuesta Park, and negotiations to buy or lease two properties on Clyde Avenue. These were closed-door discussions with city lawyers and staff to handle sensitive negotiations and legal issues.
keyTopics: Cuesta Park water main incident and possible lawsuit
Property negotiations for 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue
Legal counsel review of litigation risks

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 5 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. The agenda was light, with no major public business items listed for discussion.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not disclosed to public)
Regular council meeting convened
Small business focus area mentioned

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held an early planning meeting to review Cupertino's spending plan for the next fiscal year (July 2026 through June 2027). The meeting also covered rules about who needs to register with the city if they're trying to influence local government decisions on laws or policies.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
Rules for people attempting to influence city legislative or administrative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 11, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting focused on economic development and marketing initiatives. The city is offering both in-person and remote participation options for residents who want to attend or comment on these discussions.
keyTopics: Economic Development & Marketing Committee meeting
In-person and remote participation via Zoom
Public comment submission deadline (11 AM day of meeting)
Contact: mayorandcouncil@santaclaraca.gov for written comments

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to approve the city's spending plans for the next five years and discussed the budget for the upcoming fiscal year. They also approved a master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: 5-year Capital Improvement Plan (2027-2031) approved
2026-27 Operating Budget presentation and discussion
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The city council held a hearing about a plan to build a new 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue. The building would be 100% affordable housing and would replace the current city parking lot (Lot T) on that site. Because it's affordable housing, the developer is asking the city for some rule waivers to make the project work.
keyTopics: 72 new affordable housing units on Lytton Avenue
6-story building replacing city parking lot (Lot T)
Request for zoning/rule waivers under state affordable housing law
Environmental review underway for project approval
